autonomy_budget.summary: The first real adf-dev continuity evaluation stayed within the bounded run budget, but it failed usefully on a contract mismatch rather than completing unattended continuation.

block_summary.summary: The first real adf-dev continuity evaluation stopped fail-closed because the generated remote prompt still scoped the run to starter-backlog work and the writable-surface contract rejected benchmark-generated dist/candidates artifacts.

delta_summary: ADF now has one real adf-dev continuity evaluation preserved, and it proved the next remote-autonomy work should tighten the prompt scope and writable-surface contract before another unattended run.
handoff_summary: The first real adf-dev continuity evaluation is complete and preserved.
It failed usefully because the remote prompt still targeted starter-backlog-only work and the writable-surface contract rejected legitimate benchmark-generated dist/candidates outputs.
The next move is to tighten the remote autonomy contract for real current ADF tasks before rerunning unattended evaluation.
Keep deepen_dependency_aware_playbooks as the real task candidate once the remote loop contract is fixed.
highest_risk_observation: Highest risk: it is still easy to misread a live remote Codex process as successful autonomy when the actual checkpoint contract is mismatched with the real task.

memory_tier.summary: Pack-local restart memory for the first real adf-dev continuity evaluation result.

memory_validity.summary: Low-confidence active pack restart memory with 12h freshness window.

summary_lines: Do not treat a live remote Codex process on adf-dev as proof of unattended continuity if the checkpoint contract is still mismatched.
Do not rerun the same remote loop unchanged until the prompt scope and writable-surface rules are fixed for real current ADF tasks.

recommended_next_action: Recommended next action: tighten the adf-dev remote autonomy contract for real current ADF tasks, then rerun the same bounded continuity evaluation.
summary: This restart memory captures the first real adf-dev continuity evaluation and the contract mismatches it exposed.
